personality_voice.book_baseline: Euan Abercrombie is a first-year Gryffindor who is visibly terrified and overwhelmed at the Sorting. He stares at Harry with a 'horrified' expression, whispers nervously to a blond classmate, and is described as wanting to sink through the floor. His defining trait is acute anxiety and self-consciousness in a high-pressure social moment.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: Euan Abercrombie does not appear in the film corpus at all.
personality_voice.difference: The character is entirely absent from the films.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: A score of 0 is the only appropriate score when a character has no film presence whatsoever. There is nothing to evaluate.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Euan's narrative function is minor but specific: he serves as a mirror reflecting Harry's social isolation in OotP. His horrified reaction to Harry — prompted by the Daily Prophet's smear campaign — shows that even new, impressionable students have been turned against Harry. He is a small but functional plot device illustrating Harry's alienation.
narrative_role_agency.film_portrayal: Euan Abercrombie does not appear in the film corpus.
narrative_role_agency.difference: The character and his narrative function are entirely absent.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: No film presence means no agency can be evaluated. Score is 0.

motivations_internal_conflict.book_baseline: Euan's motivation is implicit: fear of the unknown and social conformity. He has absorbed the wider wizarding world's suspicion of Harry and reacts accordingly, despite being a brand-new student.
motivations_internal_conflict.penalty_logic: Score is 0 due to complete absence.

character_arc.book_baseline: Euan has no meaningful arc. He appears in one book (OotP) in a single scene cluster. His role is static and functional rather than developmental.
character_arc.penalty_logic: Score is 0 due to complete absence. A flat arc cannot be credited as preserved when the character does not appear.

key_relationships.book_baseline: Euan has no developed relationship with any character. His only interaction is a whispered exchange with an unnamed blond classmate and a horrified glance at Harry. The Harry dynamic is the only one with any narrative weight.
key_relationships.penalty_logic: Score is 0 due to complete absence.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.book_baseline: Euan is a minor character with limited complexity. His value lies in the specific moment: a new Gryffindor who should admire Harry instead recoils from him, which concisely dramatizes Harry's social isolation in OotP.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.difference: The entire character and the nuance he provides — the irony of a new Gryffindor fearing Harry — is lost.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.penalty_logic: Score is 0 due to complete absence.

lost_or_transferred_material: Euan's horrified reaction to Harry, which illustrates the effectiveness of the Daily Prophet's smear campaign against him in OotP
The visual beat of a new Gryffindor — who should idolize Harry — being frightened of him, reinforcing Harry's alienation
The Sorting scene moment of Euan wanting to 'sink through the floor', establishing his anxious, overwhelmed characterization
score_caps_applied: No caps formally applied — score is 0 across all dimensions due to complete absence from the film corpus, not due to cap triggers
comparative.key_observations: Euan Abercrombie does not appear in the films at all. A score of 0 across all dimensions is the only defensible result. His book role is minor — a functional background character whose primary value is dramatizing Harry's social isolation in OotP — but that function is entirely absent from the films. Confidence is Low across all dimensions because the film corpus provides nothing to evaluate; the scores reflect absence, not a judgment of quality.
